pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json, setup_task.sh (line 12), and export_result.sh (line 6) - the user profile name is consistently referenced throughout all task scripts

Confirmed in setup_openbci.sh (line 17: 'mkdir -p /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings') and export_result.sh (line 7). Also confirmed by OpenBCI official documentation at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/ which states settings are stored in [USER]/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/

Referenced in export_result.sh (line 14: 'if [ -d "$SETTINGS_DIR/Users/$TARGET_USER" ]'). OpenBCI documentation at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/ confirms user profiles are stored in Users/ subdirectory within Settings/

Explicitly stated in install_openbci.sh (line 46: 'Download OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (latest stable Linux release)') and confirmed by the release URL https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/download/v5.2.2/openbcigui_v5.2.2_2023-08-21_16-14-34_linux64.zip with release date of August 21, 2023

Task description in task.json (line 5) explicitly states: 'Select SYNTHETIC (algorithmic) as the data source'. This is not using real EEG data - the task creates a user profile and selects synthetic data generation mode
Defined in env.json (lines 115-134: user account 'ga' with password 'password123'). Confirmed in setup_openbci.sh and setup_task.sh which use 'su - ga' commands
Standard Linux home directory convention for user 'ga'. Confirmed by all scripts using /home/ga paths (setup_openbci.sh line 16, export_result.sh line 13, etc.)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating a new user profile named 'Subject_Alpha' in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023) and starting a synthetic session. OpenBCI is an official, open-source EEG visualization and analysis application available at https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI. The task operates on OpenBCI's user profile system, which stores settings in /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/ and user profiles in the Users/ subdirectory. The task uses synthetic (algorithmic) data, not real EEG data. The GUI runs under the 'ga' user account on a desktop environment with DISPLAY=:1.
